spec_match.rationale: Calls both the new navigator.mediaDevices.setPreferredSinkId and the legacy HTMLMediaElement.setSinkId, and feature-detects each. Matches the spec's contract — top-frame preferred sink vs per-element override.

interactive.rationale: Real getUserMedia → enumerateDevices → two action buttons that invoke the actual APIs against a real audio element, with a live log of outcomes/errors.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles missing API and zero outputs. Doesn't show NotAllowedError from missing mic permission, or NotFoundError when the device unplugs after enumerate, or the fact that setPreferredSinkId must be called from the top-level frame (would throw in an iframe).

references.rationale: Links to chromestatus.com/feature/5098834847072256 and the W3C audio-output spec.

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout, no raw hex, audio + buttons inherit global styles correctly.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the top-frame-only constraint
openQuestions.detail: Per spec, setPreferredSinkId is restricted to the top-level browsing context — calling it from an iframe should reject. Add an in-page iframe that tries the call and shows the rejection; teaches the user that this API differs from per-element setSinkId in scope.

openQuestions.title: Use a real (non-silent) audio sample
openQuestions.detail: The src is a tiny silent WAV ('short silent clip'). Readers can't actually hear the routing work. Either embed a small public-domain tone (e.g. 1s 440Hz sine encoded inline) or generate one with Web Audio for verification.

openQuestions.title: Surface the NotAllowedError / NotFoundError cases
openQuestions.detail: Errors from getUserMedia denial, missing audio permission policy, or a device that disappears between enumerate and setPreferredSinkId all surface as catch-blocks today. Add labelled buttons that intentionally trigger these paths so the reader sees the error names.

summary: Solid baseline that exercises both the new and the legacy API with feature detection and live logging. Two gaps: it never illustrates the top-frame-only restriction (a real spec distinction) and the demo audio is silent so the routing effect can't be heard.
